source.document: PPDA Guideline 1/2014: Thresholds for Procurement Methods
source.authority: Public Procurement and Disposal of Public Assets Authority
source.legal_basis: Regulation 6(4) of PPDA (Rules and Methods for Procurement of Supplies, Works and Non-Consultancy Services) Regulations, 2014
source.disclaimer: ILLUSTRATIVE -- thresholds are subject to periodic updates by the PPDA Authority. Verify current thresholds from PPDA Authority before use in any actual procurement.

works_thresholds.method: Open Domestic Bidding or Open International Bidding
works_thresholds.notes: Default method for works above UGX 500 million. Open International Bidding when insufficient domestic competition. Guideline 1/2014, Section 1.1.

works_thresholds.method: Restricted Domestic Bidding or Restricted International Bidding
works_thresholds.notes: Selection from shortlist. Requires justification: limited providers (Regulation 9) or compatibility/continuity (Regulation 10). Guideline 1/2014, Section 1.2.
Minimum 3 quotations from shortlist. No public bid opening required. Guideline 1/2014, Section 1.3.

works_thresholds.approval: Accounting Officer (may delegate to User Department)
works_thresholds.notes: For unforeseen requirements only. No bidding document, bid submission, public opening, or evaluation committee required. Guideline 1/2014, Section 1.4.

conditions: Emergency situations (Regulation 8): urgent, unforeseeable, not caused by dilatory conduct
Limited number of providers or sole source (Regulation 9): patent, monopoly, proprietary rights
Compatibility and continuity (Regulation 10): existing works compatibility
special_methods.notes: Even in emergencies, maximum feasible competition must be sought. Consider competitive methods with modifications before direct procurement (Regulation 8(5)-(9)).
